title: catevala × hybrida
intro: Catevala × hybrida, also known as Hound's Tongue, is a flowering perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, such as its ability to aid digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Catevala × hybrida include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make remedies for various ailments, including coughs, colds, and digestive problems.
